title: Safetensors Joins PyTorch Foundation to Enhance Open-Source AI Safety
summary: Safetensors, a popular open-source library for secure model serialization, has joined the PyTorch Foundation. This move aims to improve AI safety standards and interoperability within the PyTorch ecosystem.
body:
Safetensors, known for its secure and efficient model serialization capabilities, has officially become a part of the PyTorch Foundation. This strategic move is expected to bolster the safety and reliability of AI models within the PyTorch ecosystem. The integration will allow for better standardization and wider adoption of Safetensors' protocols.

The collaboration is significant because it addresses critical concerns around AI model security. Safetensors has gained traction for its ability to prevent malicious code execution during model loading, a feature that is increasingly important as AI models become more complex and widely used. By joining forces with the PyTorch Foundation, Safetensors will benefit from greater resources and community support.

Looking ahead, this partnership is likely to accelerate the development of safer AI practices. The PyTorch Foundation's backing will help Safetensors reach more developers and organizations, potentially setting new industry standards for model serialization. The open-source community will be watching closely to see how this alliance impacts the future of AI safety and interoperability.
twitterThread:

By joining PyTorch, Safetensors gets more resources and community support. This could set new industry standards.

Let that sink in.
The wild part? This move could accelerate safer AI practices across the board. A huge win for the open-source community.

Hot take: This is a game-changer for AI safety. Expect more organizations to adopt Safetensors' protocols.

The future of AI just got a little safer.
